seo.title: Product Manager Resume Examples & ATS Templates 2026
seo.description: Learn how to write a perfect Product Manager resume that passes the ATS. Discover the top keywords, common mistakes, and view a complete 100/100 resume example.
seo.keywords: product manager resume, product management resume template, ats resume, product manager skills
seo.ogTitle: How to Write a Product Manager Resume in 2026
seo.ogDescription: Steal our exact resume template that landed roles at top tech companies.

hero.title: Product Manager Resume Examples
hero.description: Stop getting auto-rejected by the ATS. Here is the exact resume structure, keywords, and action verbs you need to land interviews in 2026.

mistakes: Using generic terms like 'responsible for' instead of action verbs like 'Managed' or 'Developed'.
Listing features instead of benefits (e.g., 'Launched a new feature' instead of 'Increased user engagement by 25% through a new feature').
Failing to quantify impact (e.g., 'Improved customer satisfaction' instead of 'Improved customer satisfaction ratings by 15% through targeted product enhancements').
tips: Tailor your resume to the specific job description, highlighting relevant skills and experiences.
Use specific metrics and data to demonstrate the impact of your work.
Include relevant certifications, such as CSPO or AIPMM, to demonstrate expertise.

exampleResume.name: Emily Chen
exampleResume.title: Product Manager
exampleResume.summary: Results-driven Product Manager with 5+ years of experience developing and launching successful products. Proven track record of driving growth through data-driven decision making and cross-functional team collaboration.

bullets: Developed and executed a product roadmap that resulted in a 30% increase in sales revenue.
Collaborated with cross-functional teams to launch a new product feature, resulting in a 25% increase in user engagement.
Conducted market analysis and competitive research to inform product development and go-to-market strategies.
Assisted in the development and launch of a new product, resulting in a 20% increase in customer acquisition.
Worked closely with the engineering team to prioritize and develop new features, resulting in a 15% increase in customer satisfaction.

exampleResume.skills: Product Development, Agile Methodologies, Product Roadmap, Market Analysis, Competitive Analysis, Customer Feedback, Data-Driven Decision Making, Cross-Functional Team Management, Prioritization, Stakeholder Management
